figures.caption: Figure 7: Comparison of the density of a Log-normal distribution with parameters (log(2), α) with the densities of Gamma distributions with parameters (ℓ∗(α), β∗(log(2), α)), (ℓ∗∗(α), β∗∗(log(2), α)) and (ℓ∗∗∗(α), β∗∗∗(log(2), α)) (see (4.10) and (4.12)), for α ∈ {0.2, 0.4}.

Figure 4: Estimation results with the estimator n̂ (N,K) 0 defined in (4.4) when b = 1, g = 1[0,1], N = 40, K = 250 and n0 ∈ {h100,120, h225,200, h324,330, h400,500}. Comparison with the results obtained with the estimator n̂ (old,N)
Figure 11: Estimation results in the same setting as in Figures 8a and 8b, except that K = 36. For plotting the curves, the numerical precision of the computations was set to 200 digits.

Figure 9: Estimation results with the estimator n (N,K,α) 0 defined in (4.13) when b = 1, g = 1[0,1], N = 40, nd ∈ {30, 3000}, for α chosen with the method nrd (at the top) or SJ-ste (at the bottom) of the package logKDE of R, and for initial Gamma distributions. Comparison with the results obtained with n̂ (old,N,α)

Figure 10: Estimation results with the estimator n (N,K,α) 0 defined in (4.13) when b = 1, g = 1[0,1], N = 40, nd = 300, for α chosen with the method SJ-ste of the package logKDE of R, for the initial distributions defined in (4.15). Comparison with the results obtained with the estimator n̂ (old,N,α)

Figure 2: Estimation results with the estimator n̂ (N,K) 0 defined in (4.4) when b = 1, g = 1[0,1], N = 40, K = 250 and n0 ∈ {h9,12, h16,16, h25,30, h49,50}. Comparison with the results obtained with the estimator n̂ (old,N)

Figure 1: Representation of the set QN (α, β) = {p ∈ C | Re(p) > α, Re(qN (p)) > β} (in red) for different values of (α, β) ∈ (R−)2, and comparison with vertical lines (in blue), when b = 1, g = 1[0,1] and N = 40. We see that as the sets (QN (α, β))(α,β)∈(R−)2 have a conical shape, they do not contain vertical lines. Since the set PN defined in (2.12) has the same form as the sets (QN (α, β))(α,β)∈(R−)2, this figure illustrates that PN does not contain an interval of the form [γ − i∞, γ + i∞], where γ ∈ R.

Figure 8: Estimation results with the estimator n (N,K,α) 0 defined in (4.13) when b = 1, g = 1[0,1], N = 40, nd = 300, for α chosen with the method SJ-ste of the package logKDE of R, and for initial Gamma distributions. Comparison with the results obtained with the estimator n̂ (old,N,α) 0 defined in (4.14) and constructed in [27], for the same value of α. For plotting the curves, the numerical precision of the computations was set to 200 digits.

Figure 5: Comparison of the absolute errors between the Laplace transform of a Log-normal distribution with parameters (log(2), α) and the Laplace transform of Gamma distributions with parameters (ℓ∗(α), β∗(log(2), α)), (ℓ∗∗(α), β∗∗(log(2), α)) and (ℓ∗∗∗(α), β∗∗∗(log(2), α)) (see (4.10) and (4.12)), when α ∈ {0.2, 0.4}.
